title: suaeda maritima var. spicata
intro: Suaeda maritima var. spicata, also known as the prickly glasswort, is a coastal succulent plant native to salt marshes and estuaries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Suaeda maritima var. spicata include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
